      */\n    char             endpoint[SC_IPC_MAX_ENDPOINT + 1];\n} sc_ipc_channel_t;\n\n/* ------------------------------------------------------------------------- */\n/*  Initialization / teardown                                                */\n/* ------------------------------------------------------------------------- */\n/*\n * Initialize a listening server socket at `endpoint` (UNIX-domain path).\n * Returns SC_IPC_SUCCESS on success, otherwise a negative error code.\n */\nint sc_ipc_server_init(sc_ipc_channel_t      *srv,\n                       const char            *endpoint,\n                       size_t                 backlog);\n\n/*\n * Initialize a client socket and connect to the specified `endpoint`.\n * `timeout_ms` ∈ [0, UINT32_MAX]  (0 → block indefinitely).\n */\nint sc_ipc_client_init(sc_ipc_channel_t      *cli,\n                       const char            *endpoint,\n                       uint32_t               timeout_ms);\n\n/*\n * Accept an incoming client connection on `srv` into `out_cli`.\n * On success returns SC_IPC_SUCCESS and `out_cli` is initialized.\n */\nint sc_ipc_accept(const sc_ipc_channel_t     *srv,\n                  sc_ipc_channel_t           *out_cli,\n                  uint32_t                    timeout_ms);\n\n/* Close socket and wipe internal state (safe to call on uninitialized obj). */\nvoid sc_ipc_close(sc_ipc_channel_t *chan);\n\n/* ------------------------------------------------------------------------- */\n/*  Message I/O                                                              */\n/* ------------------------------------------------------------------------- */\n/*\n * Send a payload with envelope fields filled automatically.  The function\n * blocks up to `timeout_ms`;  0 → blocking, UINT32_MAX → poll once + return.\n */\nint sc_ipc_send_msg(sc_ipc_channel_t        *chan,\n                    sc_ipc_msg_type_t         type,\n                    const void              *payload,\n                    size_t                    payload_len,\n                    uint64_t                  correlation_id,\n                    uint32_t                  timeout_ms);\n\n/*\n * Receive a message into `buf` (size `buf_size`) and write header to `hdr`.\n * On success returns the number of payload bytes copied into `buf`.  The\n * caller may pass NULL for `buf` to peek at the header without reading the\n * payload (useful for length negotiation).\n */\nint sc_ipc_recv_msg(sc_ipc_channel_t        *chan,\n                    sc_ipc_msg_hdr_t        *hdr,\n                    void                    *buf,\n                    size_t                   buf_size,\n                    uint32_t                 timeout_ms);\n\n/* ------------------------------------------------------------------------- */\n/*  Utility helpers                                                          */\n/* ------------------------------------------------------------------------- */\n/* Generates a random, non-zero correlation identifier. */\nuint64_t sc_ipc_gen_correlation_id(void);\n\n/* Blocking helper: drain socket until all outstanding data has been read.   */\nint sc_ipc_drain(sc_ipc_channel_t *chan);\n\n/* ------------------------------------------------------------------------- */\n/*  Inline helpers                                                           */\n/* ------------------------------------------------------------------------- */\nstatic inline bool sc_ipc_is_ok(int rv)\n{\n    return rv >= 0;\n}\n\n/* ------------------------------------------------------------------------- */\n#ifdef __cplusplus\n} /* extern \"C\" */\n#endif\n\n#endif /* SC_IPC_H */\n", "numLines": 207, "startLine": 1, "totalLines": 207}}

1→# ==================================================================================================
     2→#  SynestheticCanvas – API GraphQL Micro-Service
     3→#  CMake build script (top-level) – production ready
     4→#
     5→#  This CMakeLists.txt orchestrates the complete build, test and packaging workflow
     6→#  for the “api_graphql” micro-service written in C.  It supports a modern workflow
     7→#  with dependency fetching, compile-time feature toggles, sanitizers, code coverage,
     8→#  version auto-discovery from git and an exportable Config package.
     9→#
    10→#  Author:  SynestheticCanvas Core Team
    11→#  SPDX-License-Identifier: MIT
    12→# ==================================================================================================
    13→
    14→cmake_minimum_required (VERSION 3.18 FATAL_ERROR)
    15→
    16→# --------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
    17→#  Project metadata
    18→# --------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
    19→project (synesthetic_canvas_api_graphql
    20→        VERSION 1.4.0                                   # Semantic version *baseline*; see below
    21→        DESCRIPTION "GraphQL gateway micro-service for SynestheticCanvas constellation"
    22→        LANGUAGES C                                    # Pure C (C17 standard)
    23→        )
    24→
    25→set (SC_API_GQL_TARGET       syn_canvas_api_graphql)   # Main target name
    26→set (SC_API_GQL_SOVERSION    1)                        # For shared lib if built
    27→
    28→
    29→# --------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
    30→#  Build options
    31→# --------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
    32→option (SC_ENABLE_TESTS        "Build unit/integration tests"                       ON)
    33→option (SC_ENABLE_SANITIZERS   "Enable compile-time sanitizers (Debug only)"        ON)
    34→option (SC_ENABLE_COVERAGE     "Enable coverage instrumentation (GCC/Clang, Debug)" OFF)
    35→option (SC_BUILD_SHARED        "Build shared library instead of executable"         OFF)
    36→option (SC_WARNINGS_AS_ERRORS  "Treat compiler warnings as errors"                  ON)
    37→
    38→# --------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
    39→#  Toolchain & Compilation
    40→# --------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
    41→set (CMAKE_C_STANDARD          17)
    42→set (CMAKE_C_STANDARD_REQUIRED ON)
    43→set (CMAKE_POSITION_INDEPENDENT_CODE ON)            # For shared libs / plugins
    44→
    45→if (NOT CMAKE_BUILD_TYPE)
    46→    set (CMAKE_BUILD_TYPE Debug CACHE STRING "Build type" FORCE)
    47→endif ()
    48→
    49→# Extra strictness
    50→if (CMAKE_C_COMPILER_ID MATCHES "GNU|Clang")
    51→    add_compile_options (
    52→        -Wall -Wextra -Wpedantic
    53→        -Wcast-align -Wpointer-arith -Wstrict-prototypes
    54→        -Wmissing-prototypes -Wformat=2 -Wshadow
    55→    )
    56→    if (SC_WARNINGS_AS_ERRORS)
    57→        add_compile_options (-Werror)
    58→    endif ()
    59→elseif (MSVC)
    60→    add_compile_options (/W4 /permissive-)
    61→endif ()
    62→
    63→
    64→# --------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
    65→#  Version auto-discovery from git (overrides project() version if available)
    66→# --------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
    67→# Generates include/sc_version.h with:
    68→#     #define SC_API_GQL_VERSION "1.4.0+g<commit>"
    69→#     #define SC_API_GQL_GIT_HASH "<commit>"
    70→#     #define SC_API_GQL_GIT_BRANCH "<branch>"
    71→# --------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
    72→find_package (Git QUIET)
    73→
    74→if (GIT_FOUND AND EXISTS "${PROJECT_SOURCE_DIR}/.git")
    75→    execute_process (COMMAND ${GIT_EXECUTABLE} rev-parse --short HEAD
    76→                     OUTPUT_VARIABLE GIT_HASH OUTPUT_STRIP_TRAILING_WHITESPACE)
    77→    execute_process (COMMAND ${GIT_EXECUTABLE} rev-parse --abbrev-ref HEAD
    78→                     OUTPUT_VARIABLE GIT_BRANCH OUTPUT_STRIP_TRAILING_WHITESPACE)
    79→    set (PROJECT_VERSION "${PROJECT_VERSION}+g${GIT_HASH}")
    80→endif ()
    81→
    82→configure_file (
    83→    ${CMAKE_CURRENT_SOURCE_DIR}/cmake/templates/sc_version.h.in
    84→    ${CMAKE_CURRENT_BINARY_DIR}/generated/sc_version.h
    85→    @ONLY
    86→)
    87→
    88→include_directories (${CMAKE_CURRENT_BINARY_DIR}/generated)
    89→install (FILES ${CMAKE_CURRENT_BINARY_DIR}/generated/sc_version.h
    90→        DESTINATION include/synesthetic_canvas)
    91→
    92→
    93→# --------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
    94→#  Dependencies
    95→# --------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
    96→include (FetchContent)
    97→
    98→# 1. cJSON – Lightweight JSON parser/serializer
    99→FetchContent_Declare (
   100→    cJSON
   101→    GIT_REPOSITORY https://github.com/DaveGamble/cJSON.git
   102→    GIT_TAG        v1.7.16
   103→)
   104→FetchContent_MakeAvailable (cJSON)
   105→
   106→# 2. libgraphqlparser – Facebook’s GraphQL parser (C bindings)
   107→#    Pre-built packages may not exist everywhere, so fallback to FetchContent
   108→find_package (graphqlparser QUIET)
   109→if (NOT graphqlparser_FOUND)
   110→    message (STATUS "libgraphqlparser not found, fetching from source…")
   111→    FetchContent_Declare (
   112→        graphqlparser
   113→        GIT_REPOSITORY https://github.com/graphql/libgraphqlparser.git
   114→        GIT_TAG        0.7.2
   115→    )
   116→    set (BUILD_SHARED_LIBS OFF CACHE BOOL "" FORCE)        # Build static internally
   117→    FetchContent_MakeAvailable (graphqlparser)
   118→endif ()
   119→
   120→# 3. libuv – Event loop / async I/O abstraction
   121→find_package (uv 1.30 REQUIRED)
   122→
   123→# 4. OpenSSL (for TLS if we add HTTPS listener)
   124→find_package (OpenSSL 1.1 REQUIRED)
   125→
   126→# 5. zlib (for compressed payloads)
   127→find_package (ZLIB REQUIRED)
   128→
   129→
   130→# --------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
   131→#  Source layout
   132→# --------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
   133→# We keep a flat list for clarity; you may generate this automatically with filesystem globbing, but
   134→# explicit listing is preferred for reproducible builds.
   135→set (SC_API_GQL_SRC
   136→    src/main.c
   137→    src/http_server.c
   138→    src/http_server.h
   139→    src/graphql_router.c
   140→    src/graphql_router.h
   141→    src/validators.c
   142→    src/validators.h
   143→    src/pagination.c
   144→    src/pagination.h
   145→    src/logging.c
   146→    src/logging.h
   147→    src/metrics.c
   148→    src/metrics.h
   149→    src/config.c
   150→    src/config.h
   151→)
   152→
   153→# --------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
   154→#  Build target
   155→# --------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
   156→if (SC_BUILD_SHARED)
   157→    add_library      (${SC_API_GQL_TARGET} SHARED ${SC_API_GQL_SRC})
   158→    set_target_properties (${SC_API_GQL_TARGET}
   159→        PROPERTIES SOVERSION ${SC_API_GQL_SOVERSION})
   160→else ()
   161→    add_executable   (${SC_API_GQL_TARGET} ${SC_API_GQL_SRC})
   162→endif ()
   163→
   164→target_include_directories (${SC_API_GQL_TARGET}
   165→    PUBLIC  $<BUILD_INTERFACE:${PROJECT_SOURCE_DIR}/include>
   166→            $<INSTALL_INTERFACE:include>
   167→)
   168→
   169→target_link_libraries (${SC_API_GQL_TARGET}
   170→    PRIVATE
   171→        cjson
   172→        graphqlparser
   173→        uv
   174→        OpenSSL::SSL
   175→        ZLIB::ZLIB
   176→)
   177→
   178→# --------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
   179→#  Sanitizers (clang/gcc, Debug only)
   180→# --------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
   181→if (SC_ENABLE_SANITIZERS AND CMAKE_BUILD_TYPE STREQUAL "Debug")
   182→    if (CMAKE_C_COMPILER_ID MATCHES "GNU|Clang")
   183→        target_compile_options (${SC_API_GQL_TARGET} PRIVATE -fsanitize=address,undefined -fno-omit-frame-pointer)
   184→        target_link_options    (${SC_API_GQL_TARGET} PRIVATE -fsanitize=address,undefined)
   185→    endif ()
   186→endif ()
   187→
   188→# --------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
   189→#  Coverage
   190→# --------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
   191→if (SC_ENABLE_COVERAGE AND CMAKE_C_COMPILER_ID MATCHES "GNU|Clang")
   192→    target_compile_options (${SC_API_GQL_TARGET} PRIVATE -O0 -g --coverage)
   193→    target_link_options    (${SC_API_GQL_TARGET} PRIVATE --coverage)
   194→endif ()
   195→
   196→
   197→# --------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
   198→#  Install rules
   199→# --------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
   200→include (GNUInstallDirs)
   201→
   202→install (TARGETS ${SC_API_GQL_TARGET}
   203→        EXPORT  SynestheticCanvasTargets
   204→        RUNTIME DESTINATION ${CMAKE_INSTALL_BINDIR}
   205→        LIBRARY DESTINATION ${CMAKE_INSTALL_LIBDIR}
   206→        ARCHIVE DESTINATION ${CMAKE_INSTALL_LIBDIR}
   207→)
   208→
   209→install (DIRECTORY include/
   210→        DESTINATION ${CMAKE_INSTALL_INCLUDEDIR}
   211→        FILES_MATCHING PATTERN "*.h"
   212→)
   213→
   214→#  Export a pkg-config file
   215→configure_file (
   216→    ${CMAKE_CURRENT_SOURCE_DIR}/cmake/templates/synesthetic_canvas_api_graphql.pc.in
   217→    ${CMAKE_CURRENT_BINARY_DIR}/synesthetic_canvas_api_graphql.pc
   218→    @ONLY
   219→)
   220→install (FILES ${CMAKE_CURRENT_BINARY_DIR}/synesthetic_canvas_api_graphql.pc
   221→        DESTINATION ${CMAKE_INSTALL_LIBDIR}/pkgconfig)
   222→
   223→#  CMake package config for find_package()
   224→install (EXPORT SynestheticCanvasTargets
   225→        FILE SynestheticCanvasTargets.cmake
   226→        NAMESPACE SynestheticCanvas::
   227→        DESTINATION ${CMAKE_INSTALL_LIBDIR}/cmake/SynestheticCanvas)
   228→
   229→include (CMakePackageConfigHelpers)
   230→configure_package_config_file (
   231→    ${CMAKE_CURRENT_SOURCE_DIR}/cmake/templates/SynestheticCanvasConfig.cmake.in
   232→    ${CMAKE_CURRENT_BINARY_DIR}/SynestheticCanvasConfig.cmake
   233→    INSTALL_DESTINATION ${CMAKE_INSTALL_LIBDIR}/cmake/SynestheticCanvas
   234→)
   235→install (FILES
   236→    ${CMAKE_CURRENT_BINARY_DIR}/SynestheticCanvasConfig.cmake
   237→    DESTINATION ${CMAKE_INSTALL_LIBDIR}/cmake/SynestheticCanvas
   238→)
   239→
   240→# --------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
   241→#  Testing
   242→# --------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
   243→if (SC_ENABLE_TESTS)
   244→    enable_testing ()
   245→    add_subdirectory (tests)
   246→endif ()
   247→
   248→
   249→# --------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
   250→#  Developer utilities
   251→# --------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
   252→#   make clang-format / make lint / etc.
   253→add_custom_target (clang-format
   254→    COMMAND clang-format -i ${SC_API_GQL_SRC}
   255→    WORKING_DIRECTORY ${PROJECT_SOURCE_DIR}
   256→    COMMENT "Running clang-format on source tree"
   257→)
   258→
   259→#  Doxygen documentation (if Doxygen present)
   260→find_package (Doxygen OPTIONAL_COMPONENTS dot)
   261→if (DOXYGEN_FOUND)
   262→    set (DOXYGEN_GENERATE_LATEX NO)
   263→    doxygen_add_docs (doc
   264→        ${PROJECT_SOURCE_DIR}/include
   265→        ${PROJECT_SOURCE_DIR}/src
   266→        COMMENT "Generating API documentation with Doxygen"
   267→    )
   268→endif ()
   269→
   270→
   271→# --------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
   272→#  Summaries
   273→# --------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
   274→message (STATUS "")
   275→message (STATUS "==============================================================")
   276→message (STATUS "  SynestheticCanvas – api_graphql")
   277→message (STATUS "  Version          : ${PROJECT_VERSION}")
   278→message (STATUS "  Build Type       : ${CMAKE_BUILD_TYPE}")
   279→message (STATUS "  Sanitizers       : ${SC_ENABLE_SANITIZERS}")
   280→message (STATUS "  Coverage         : ${SC_ENABLE_COVERAGE}")
   281→message (STATUS "  Tests            : ${SC_ENABLE_TESTS}")

     2→# SynestheticCanvas/api-gateway – CMakeLists.txt
     3→#
     4→# This top-level build script configures, builds, tests and packages the
     5→# “api-gateway” component of the SynestheticCanvas API Suite.
     6→#
     7→# Highlights
     8→# ----------
     9→# • Out-of-source, target-based CMake (>=3.20) with modern C standards (C17)
    10→# • Automatic version detection from Git tags + “overridable” build timestamp
    11→# • Strict compile flags + optional sanitizers           (ENABLE_SANITIZERS=ON)
    12→# • Static analysis with clang-tidy & cppcheck           (ENABLE_ANALYSIS=ON)
    13→# • Flexible dependency discovery (system, FetchContent, vendor fall-back)
    14→# • Unit/Integration tests via CTest + Coverage          (ENABLE_COVERAGE=ON)
    15→# • Install rules & CPACK packaging for DEB/RPM/TGZ
    16→# • API gateway can be built as shared lib and CLI tool  (BUILD_CLI=ON)
    17→# ====================================================================================
    18→
    19→cmake_minimum_required(VERSION 3.20 FATAL_ERROR)
    20→project(api_gateway
    21→        VERSION 1.4.0                       # fallback version (overridden by git)
    22→        DESCRIPTION "SynestheticCanvas GraphQL / REST API gateway"
    23→        LANGUAGES C)
    24→
    25→# -----------------------------------------------------------------------------
    26→# Options
    27→# -----------------------------------------------------------------------------
    28→option(BUILD_SHARED_LIBS  "Build shared library instead of static" ON)
    29→option(BUILD_CLI          "Build the standalone gateway executable" ON)
    30→option(ENABLE_TESTS       "Build unit & integration tests"          ON)
    31→option(ENABLE_SANITIZERS  "Enable address/undefined sanitizers"     OFF)
    32→option(ENABLE_COVERAGE    "Enable coverage instrumentation"         OFF)
    33→option(ENABLE_ANALYSIS    "Run static analysis tools at build time" OFF)
    34→
    35→# -----------------------------------------------------------------------------
    36→# Project version (prefer Git tags if available)
    37→# -----------------------------------------------------------------------------
    38→include(CheckIPOSupported)
    39→include(GNUInstallDirs)
    40→include(CMakePackageConfigHelpers)
    41→include(CTest)
    42→
    43→find_program(GIT_EXECUTABLE git)
    44→if(GIT_EXECUTABLE)
    45→    execute_process(
    46→        COMMAND ${GIT_EXECUTABLE} describe --tags --abbrev=0
    47→        WORKING_DIRECTORY ${CMAKE_CURRENT_SOURCE_DIR}
    48→        OUTPUT_VARIABLE _GIT_TAG
    49→        OUTPUT_STRIP_TRAILING_WHITESPACE
    50→        ERROR_QUIET)
    51→    if(_GIT_TAG)
    52→        message(STATUS "Project version derived from git: ${_GIT_TAG}")
    53→        set(PROJECT_VERSION "${_GIT_TAG}" CACHE STRING "Project version" FORCE)
    54→    endif()
    55→endif()
    56→
    57→# -----------------------------------------------------------------------------
    58→# Build type & default flags
    59→# -----------------------------------------------------------------------------
    60→if(NOT CMAKE_BUILD_TYPE)
    61→    set(CMAKE_BUILD_TYPE "RelWithDebInfo" CACHE STRING
    62→        "Build type (Debug, Release, RelWithDebInfo, MinSizeRel)" FORCE)
    63→endif()
    64→
    65→set(C_API_STANDARD 17)
    66→set(CMAKE_C_STANDARD ${C_API_STANDARD})
    67→set(CMAKE_C_STANDARD_REQUIRED ON)
    68→
    69→# Strict compilation flags
    70→set(__WARN_FLAGS
    71→    -Wall -Wextra -Wpedantic -Werror
    72→    -Wshadow -Wconversion -Wdouble-promotion -Wformat=2
    73→)
    74→if(MSVC)
    75→    add_compile_options(/W4 /WX)
    76→else()
    77→    add_compile_options(${__WARN_FLAGS})
    78→endif()
    79→
    80→# Optional sanitizers
    81→if(ENABLE_SANITIZERS AND NOT MSVC)

    83→    add_link_options    (-fsanitize=address,undefined)
    84→endif()

    92→# -----------------------------------------------------------------------------

    95→include(FetchContent)

    97→# GraphQL C library (graphql-c)

   103→FetchContent_MakeAvailable(graphql-c)

   107→    cjson
   108→    GIT_REPOSITORY https://github.com/DaveGamble/cJSON.git
   109→    GIT_TAG        v1.7.17
   110→)
   111→FetchContent_MakeAvailable(cjson)

   115→pkg_check_modules(LIBUV libuv REQUIRED)

   117→# -------------------------------------------------------------------------

   120→file(GLOB_RECURSE API_GATEWAY_SRC CONFIGURE_DEPENDS
   121→     "src/*.c" "src/*.h" "include/*.h")

   127→# -------------------------------------------------------------------------

   129→# -------------------------------------------------------------------------
   130→add_library(api_gateway ${API_GATEWAY_SRC})
   131→add_library(SynestheticCanvas::api_gateway ALIAS api_gateway)
   132→
   133→target_include_directories(api_gateway
   134→    PUBLIC
   135→        $<BUILD_INTERFACE:${CMAKE_CURRENT_SOURCE_DIR}/include>
   136→        $<INSTALL_INTERFACE:${CMAKE_INSTALL_INCLUDEDIR}>
   137→        ${graphql-c_SOURCE_DIR}/src

   139→        ${LIBUV_INCLUDE_DIRS}

   141→
   142→target_link_libraries(api_gateway

   149→# LTO/IPO
   150→check_ipo_supported(RESULT ipo_supported OUTPUT ipo_error)
   151→if(ipo_supported)
   152→    set_target_properties(api_gateway PROPERTIES INTERPROCEDURAL_OPTIMIZATION ON)
   153→endif()
   154→
   155→# -------------------------------------------------------------------------
   156→# CLI Application Target
   157→# -------------------------------------------------------------------------
   158→if(BUILD_CLI)
   159→    add_executable(api_gateway_cli src/main.c)
   160→    target_link_libraries(api_gateway_cli PRIVATE api_gateway)
   161→    set_target_properties(api_gateway_cli PROPERTIES OUTPUT_NAME "synesthetic-gw")
   162→    install(TARGETS api_gateway_cli
   163→        RUNTIME DESTINATION ${CMAKE_INSTALL_BINDIR})
   164→endif()
   165→
   166→# -------------------------------------------------------------------------
   167→# Tests
   168→# -------------------------------------------------------------------------
   169→if(ENABLE_TESTS)
   170→    enable_testing()
   171→
   172→    # FetchCriterion for lightweight unit tests
   173→    FetchContent_Declare(
   174→        criterion
   175→        GIT_REPOSITORY https://github.com/Snaipe/Criterion.git
   176→        GIT_TAG        v2.3.3
   177→    )
   178→    FetchContent_MakeAvailable(criterion)
   179→
   180→    file(GLOB_RECURSE TEST_SOURCES CONFIGURE_DEPENDS "tests/*.c")
   181→    add_executable(api_gateway_tests ${TEST_SOURCES})
   182→    target_link_libraries(api_gateway_tests PRIVATE api_gateway criterion)
   183→
   184→    add_test(NAME api_gateway_unit COMMAND api_gateway_tests)
   185→endif()
   186→
   187→# -------------------------------------------------------------------------
   188→# Static Analysis
   189→# -------------------------------------------------------------------------
   190→if(ENABLE_ANALYSIS)
   191→    find_program(CLANG_TIDY_EXE NAMES clang-tidy)

   199→            ${CPPCHECK_EXE};
   200→            --enable=warning,style,performance,portability;
   201→            --error-exitcode=1;
   202→            --inline-suppr
   203→        )
   204→    endif()
   205→endif()

   215→        LIBRARY DESTINATION ${CMAKE_INSTALL_LIBDIR}
   216→        RUNTIME DESTINATION ${CMAKE_INSTALL_BINDIR}
   217→)
   218→install(DIRECTORY include/ DESTINATION ${CMAKE_INSTALL_INCLUDEDIR})

   221→export(EXPORT api_gatewayTargets
   222→       FILE "${CMAKE_CURRENT_BINARY_DIR}/api_gatewayTargets.cmake"
   223→       NAMESPACE SynestheticCanvas::)
   224→
   225→configure_package_config_file(
   226→    cmake/api_gatewayConfig.cmake.in
   227→    "${CMAKE_CURRENT_BINARY_DIR}/api_gatewayConfig.cmake"
   228→    INSTALL_DESTINATION ${INSTALL_CONFIGDIR}
   229→)

   235→
   236→install(EXPORT api_gatewayTargets

   243→        "${CMAKE_CURRENT_BINARY_DIR}/api_gatewayConfigVersion.cmake"

     4→#  CMake build configuration for the “texture-service” micro-service. This
     5→#  service is responsible for dynamic GPU/CPU texture synthesis and format

     7→#  that is consumed by the GraphQL/REST façade layer and by the internal

    16→#    • Unit tests via CTest + Unity
    17→#    • Installation + export of CMake package config files
    18→# ========================================================================
    19→
    20→cmake_minimum_required(VERSION 3.20)
    21→
    22→# ------------------------------------------------------------------------
    23→#  Project metadata

    25→project(texture_service
    26→        VERSION 1.2.0
    27→        DESCRIPTION "Dynamic texture synthesis micro-service"
    28→        LANGUAGES C)

    30→# ------------------------------------------------------------------------

    55→set(TEXTURESVC_WARN_FLAGS
    56→        -Wall -Wextra -Wpedantic -Werror
    57→        -Wno-unused-parameter          # allowed in callbacks
    58→        -Wno-missing-field-initializers)

    60→if (MSVC)

   112→FetchContent_Declare(

   173→    file(GLOB_RECURSE TEST_SRC CONFIGURE_DEPENDS tests/*.c)

   175→    target_link_libraries(texture_service_tests PRIVATE texture_service unity)
   176→

1→#ifndef SC_CONFIG_H
     2→#define SC_CONFIG_H
     3→/*
     4→ * sc_config.h
     5→ * SynestheticCanvas Common Configuration Header
     6→ *
     7→ * This header centralises compile-time configuration toggles, version
     8→ * information, attribute/visibility helpers and a thin shim that allows
     9→ * the rest of the code-base to query runtime parameters (usually sourced
    10→ * from environment variables) without dragging in the heavyweight service
    11→ * layer.
    12→ *
    13→ *  Copyright (c) 2024
    14→ *  SynestheticCanvas Contributors <opensource@synestheticcanvas.io>
    15→ *
    16→ *  SPDX-License-Identifier: MIT
    17→ */
    18→
    19→#ifdef __cplusplus
    20→extern "C" {
    21→#endif
    22→
    23→/*==========================================================================*/
    24→/*  Standard Library Includes                                               */
    25→/*==========================================================================*/
    26→#include <stddef.h>
    27→#include <stdint.h>
    28→#include <stdbool.h>
    29→
    30→#if defined(__STDC_VERSION__) && (__STDC_VERSION__ >= 201112L)
    31→#   include <stdalign.h>
    32→#   define SC_HAS_C11 1
    33→#else
    34→#   define SC_HAS_C11 0
    35→#endif
    36→
    37→/*==========================================================================*/
    38→/*  Versioning                                                              */
    39→/*==========================================================================*/
    40→#define SC_VERSION_MAJOR    1
    41→#define SC_VERSION_MINOR    4
    42→#define SC_VERSION_PATCH    2
    43→
    44→#define SC_VERSION_STR_HELPER(x) #x
    45→#define SC_VERSION_STR(maj, min, pat)  \
    46→        SC_VERSION_STR_HELPER(maj) "." \
    47→        SC_VERSION_STR_HELPER(min) "." \
    48→        SC_VERSION_STR_HELPER(pat)
    49→
    50→#define SC_VERSION  SC_VERSION_STR(SC_VERSION_MAJOR, SC_VERSION_MINOR, SC_VERSION_PATCH)
    51→
    52→/*==========================================================================*/
    53→/*  Platform / Compiler Detection                                           */
    54→/*==========================================================================*/
    55→#if defined(_WIN32) || defined(_WIN64)
    56→#   define SC_PLATFORM_WINDOWS 1
    57→#else
    58→#   define SC_PLATFORM_WINDOWS 0
    59→#endif
    60→
    61→#if defined(__linux__)
    62→#   define SC_PLATFORM_LINUX 1
    63→#else
    64→#   define SC_PLATFORM_LINUX 0
    65→#endif
    66→
    67→#if defined(__APPLE__)
    68→#   define SC_PLATFORM_APPLE 1
    69→#else
    70→#   define SC_PLATFORM_APPLE 0
    71→#endif
    72→
    73→#if defined(__GNUC__)
    74→#   define SC_COMPILER_GCC 1
    75→#else
    76→#   define SC_COMPILER_GCC 0
    77→#endif
    78→
    79→#if defined(_MSC_VER)
    80→#   define SC_COMPILER_MSVC 1
    81→#else
    82→#   define SC_COMPILER_MSVC 0
    83→#endif
    84→
    85→/*==========================================================================*/
    86→/*  Dynamic Library Export / Import Helpers                                 */
    87→/*==========================================================================*/
    88→#if SC_PLATFORM_WINDOWS
    89→#   ifdef SC_EXPORT_SYMBOLS
    90→#       define SC_PUBLIC __declspec(dllexport)
    91→#   else
    92→#       define SC_PUBLIC __declspec(dllimport)
    93→#   endif
    94→#   define SC_PRIVATE
    95→#else
    96→#   if SC_COMPILER_GCC
    97→#       define SC_PUBLIC  __attribute__((visibility("default")))
    98→#       define SC_PRIVATE __attribute__((visibility("hidden")))
    99→#   else
   100→#       define SC_PUBLIC
   101→#       define SC_PRIVATE
   102→#   endif
   103→#endif
   104→
   105→/*==========================================================================*/
   106→/*  Static / Compile-time Assertions                                        */
   107→/*==========================================================================*/
   108→#if SC_HAS_C11
   109→#   define SC_STATIC_ASSERT(cond, msg) _Static_assert(cond, msg)
   110→#else
   111→    /* Fallback that creates an invalid typedef if the condition is false */
   112→#   define SC_STATIC_ASSERT(cond, msg) typedef char static_assertion_##msg[(cond) ? 1 : -1]
   113→#endif
   114→
   115→/* Validate fundamental assumptions early */
   116→SC_STATIC_ASSERT(sizeof(void*) == 4 || sizeof(void*) == 8, pointer_size_must_be_32_or_64_bits);
   117→
   118→/*==========================================================================*/
   119→/*  Feature Flags (Override at compile-time with -Dflag=0|1)                */
   120→/*==========================================================================*/
   121→
   122→#ifndef SC_FEATURE_GRAPHQL
   123→#   define SC_FEATURE_GRAPHQL      1
   124→#endif
   125→
   126→#ifndef SC_FEATURE_REST
   127→#   define SC_FEATURE_REST         1
   128→#endif
   129→
   130→#ifndef SC_FEATURE_VALIDATION
   131→#   define SC_FEATURE_VALIDATION   1
   132→#endif
   133→
   134→#ifndef SC_FEATURE_MONITORING
   135→#   define SC_FEATURE_MONITORING   1
   136→#endif
   137→
   138→#ifndef SC_FEATURE_LOGGING
   139→#   define SC_FEATURE_LOGGING      1
   140→#endif
   141→
   142→/*==========================================================================*/
   143→/*  Default Limits (Adjust per deployment)                                  */
   144→/*==========================================================================*/
   145→#ifndef SC_MAX_HTTP_HEADER_SIZE
   146→#   define SC_MAX_HTTP_HEADER_SIZE     (16 * 1024)   /* 16 KiB */
   147→#endif
   148→
   149→#ifndef SC_MAX_PAYLOAD_SIZE
   150→#   define SC_MAX_PAYLOAD_SIZE         (10 * 1024 * 1024) /* 10 MiB */
   151→#endif
   152→
   153→#ifndef SC_DEFAULT_PAGE_SIZE
   154→#   define SC_DEFAULT_PAGE_SIZE        50
   155→#endif
   156→
   157→/*==========================================================================*/
   158→/*  Logging                                                                 */
   159→/*==========================================================================*/
   160→typedef enum sc_log_level {
   161→    SC_LOG_TRACE = 0,
   162→    SC_LOG_DEBUG,
   163→    SC_LOG_INFO,
   164→    SC_LOG_WARN,
   165→    SC_LOG_ERROR,
   166→    SC_LOG_FATAL
   167→} sc_log_level_t;
   168→
   169→/* Compile-time default, can be overridden via -DSC_LOG_LEVEL */
   170→#ifndef SC_LOG_LEVEL
   171→#   ifdef NDEBUG
   172→#       define SC_LOG_LEVEL SC_LOG_INFO
   173→#   else
   174→#       define SC_LOG_LEVEL SC_LOG_DEBUG
   175→#   endif
   176→#endif
   177→
   178→/*==========================================================================*/
   179→/*  Runtime Configuration Structure                                         */
   180→/*==========================================================================*/
   181→typedef struct sc_runtime_config {
   182→    const char      *environment;       /* "development", "staging", "production"      */
   183→    const char      *service_name;      /* e.g., "palette-service"                     */
   184→    uint16_t         service_port;      /* default 0 means "use environment variable"   */
   185→
   186→    /* feature toggles */
   187→    bool             enable_monitoring;
   188→    bool             enable_validation;
   189→    bool             enable_request_tracing;
   190→
   191→    /* runtime knobs */
   192→    sc_log_level_t   log_level;
   193→    uint32_t         max_payload_size;
   194→    uint16_t         default_page_size;
   195→} sc_runtime_config_t;
   196→
   197→/*==========================================================================*/
   198→/*  Inline Helpers                                                          */
   199→/*==========================================================================*/
   200→#include <stdlib.h>
   201→#include <string.h>
   202→
   203→SC_PUBLIC
   204→static inline uint16_t sc_u16_from_env(const char *name, uint16_t fallback)
   205→{
   206→    const char *val = getenv(name);
   207→    if (!val || !*val) return fallback;
   208→
   209→    char *endptr = NULL;
   210→    unsigned long parsed = strtoul(val, &endptr, 10);
   211→    if (endptr == val || *endptr != '\0' || parsed > 0xFFFFUL)
   212→        return fallback;
   213→
   214→    return (uint16_t)parsed;
   215→}
   216→
   217→SC_PUBLIC
   218→static inline bool sc_bool_from_env(const char *name, bool fallback)
   219→{
   220→    const char *val = getenv(name);
   221→    if (!val || !*val) return fallback;
   222→
   223→    if (!strcasecmp(val, "1") ||
   224→        !strcasecmp(val, "true") ||
   225→        !strcasecmp(val, "yes") ||
   226→        !strcasecmp(val, "on"))
   227→        return true;
   228→
   229→    if (!strcasecmp(val, "0") ||
   230→        !strcasecmp(val, "false") ||
   231→        !strcasecmp(val, "no") ||
   232→        !strcasecmp(val, "off"))
   233→        return false;
   234→
   235→    return fallback;
   236→}
   237→
   238→SC_PUBLIC
   239→static inline sc_log_level_t sc_log_level_from_env(const char *name, sc_log_level_t fallback)
   240→{
   241→    const char *val = getenv(name);
   242→    if (!val) return fallback;
   243→
   244→    if (!strcasecmp(val, "trace")) return SC_LOG_TRACE;
   245→    if (!strcasecmp(val, "debug")) return SC_LOG_DEBUG;
   246→    if (!strcasecmp(val, "info"))  return SC_LOG_INFO;
   247→    if (!strcasecmp(val, "warn"))  return SC_LOG_WARN;
   248→    if (!strcasecmp(val, "error")) return SC_LOG_ERROR;
   249→    if (!strcasecmp(val, "fatal")) return SC_LOG_FATAL;
   250→
   251→    return fallback;
   252→}
   253→
   254→/*-------------------------------------------------------------------------
   255→ * sc_config_load_default
   256→ *
   257→ * Populate config with sane defaults, reading from well-known environment
   258→ * variables when present. Does not allocate memory – string pointers point
   259→ * directly to getenv-backed storage, the caller must copy if longer
   260→ * lifetimes are required.
   261→ *------------------------------------------------------------------------*/
   262→SC_PUBLIC
   263→static inline void sc_config_load_default(sc_runtime_config_t *cfg,
   264→                                          const char *service_name,
   265→                                          uint16_t default_port)
   266→{
   267→    if (!cfg) return;
   268→
   269→    cfg->environment           = getenv("SC_ENVIRONMENT") ? getenv("SC_ENVIRONMENT") : "development";
   270→    cfg->service_name          = service_name;
   271→    cfg->service_port          = sc_u16_from_env("SC_SERVICE_PORT", default_port);
   272→
   273→    cfg->enable_monitoring     = sc_bool_from_env("SC_ENABLE_MONITORING", SC_FEATURE_MONITORING);
   274→    cfg->enable_validation     = sc_bool_from_env("SC_ENABLE_VALIDATION", SC_FEATURE_VALIDATION);
   275→    cfg->enable_request_tracing= sc_bool_from_env("SC_ENABLE_TRACING",    false);
   276→
   277→    cfg->log_level             = sc_log_level_from_env("SC_LOG_LEVEL", (sc_log_level_t)SC_LOG_LEVEL);
   278→    cfg->max_payload_size      = (uint32_t)strtoul(getenv("SC_MAX_PAYLOAD") ?: "", NULL, 10);
   279→    if (cfg->max_payload_size == 0 || cfg->max_payload_size > SC_MAX_PAYLOAD_SIZE)
   280→        cfg->max_payload_size = SC_MAX_PAYLOAD_SIZE;
   281→
   282→    cfg->default_page_size     = (uint16_t)strtoul(getenv("SC_DEFAULT_PAGE_SIZE") ?: "", NULL, 10);
   283→    if (cfg->default_page_size == 0 || cfg->default_page_size > 1000)
   284→        cfg->default_page_size = SC_DEFAULT_PAGE_SIZE;
   285→}
   286→
   287→/*==========================================================================*/
   288→/*  Deprecation / Unavailable                                               */
   289→/*==========================================================================*/
   290→#if SC_COMPILER_GCC || (__has_attribute(deprecated))
   291→#   define SC_DEPRECATED(msg) __attribute__((deprecated(msg)))
   292→#elif SC_COMPILER_MSVC
   293→#   define SC_DEPRECATED(msg) __declspec(deprecated(msg))
   294→#else
   295→#   define SC_DEPRECATED(msg)
   296→#endif
   297→
   298→#ifdef __cplusplus
   299→} /* extern "C" */
   300→#endif
   301→
   302→#endif /* SC_CONFIG_H */
   303→

arguments.content:
# Technical Report: Migration from Custom IPC to gRPC

## Executive Summary

This report provides a comprehensive architectural analysis of the SynestheticCanvas API Suite's inter-service communication system and outlines a phased migration plan from the current hybrid IPC/HTTP approach to gRPC. The analysis is based on examination of the codebase, including the `sc_ipc` library, API Gateway, microservices, and build configuration files.

---

## 1. Component Identification

### 1.1 Current Inter-Service Communication Components

The SynestheticCanvas system uses a **hybrid communication approach**:

1. **UNIX Domain Socket IPC Library (`sc_ipc`)** - for local inter-process communication
2. **HTTP/REST over libcurl** - for network-based service-to-service calls

#### Core IPC Library Files

| File | Purpose |
|------|---------|
| `libs/sc_ipc/include/sc_ipc.h` | Public API header defining the IPC interface, message types, error codes, and envelope structures |
| `libs/sc_ipc/src/sc_ipc.c` | Implementation of UNIX domain socket-based IPC with length-prefixed framing |

#### API Gateway Client-Side Communication

| File | Purpose |
|------|---------|
| `api-gateway/src/services/service_client.c` | HTTP/REST client wrapper using libcurl for communicating with downstream microservices |
| `api-gateway/graphql/resolvers.c` | GraphQL resolvers that invoke `service_client` to call backend services |
| `api-gateway/src/main.c` | Gateway entry point and HTTP server initialization |
| `api-gateway/src/gateway_server.c` | HTTP server implementation |

#### Microservice Server-Side Components

| Service | Main Files |
|---------|-----------|
| **texture-service** | `services/texture-service/src/main.c` - HTTP daemon using libmicrohttpd |
| | `services/texture-service/src/texture_handler.c` - Request handling and business logic |
| | `services/texture-service/src/texture_service.c` - Core texture synthesis logic |
| | `services/texture-service/src/texture_repository.c` - Data persistence layer |
| **palette-service** | Similar structure in `services/palette-service/` |
| **audio-service** | Similar structure in `services/audio-service/` |
| **narrative-service** | Similar structure in `services/narrative-service/` |

#### Shared Libraries

| File | Purpose |
|------|---------|
| `libs/sc_common/include/sc_config.h` | Runtime configuration, feature flags, environment variable parsing |
| `libs/sc_common/include/sc_logging.h` | Structured logging utilities |
| `libs/sc_common/include/sc_errors.h` | Error handling framework |
| `libs/sc_common/include/sc_types.h` | Common type definitions |

### 1.2 Current Communication Architecture

Based on code analysis:

1. **The `sc_ipc` library** provides UNIX domain socket communication with:
   - Length-prefixed (4-byte big-endian) message framing
   - Non-blocking I/O with configurable timeouts
   - Server/client connection patterns
   - Maximum 64KB payload size

2. **The `service_client.c`** in the API Gateway uses:
   - libcurl for HTTP/JSON REST calls to microservices
   - Environment variables for service URLs (e.g., `SC_TEXTURE_SERVICE_URL`)
   - Exponential backoff retry logic
   - cJSON for JSON parsing

3. **Microservices** expose HTTP endpoints via libmicrohttpd:
   - REST endpoints like `/healthz`, `/v1/noise`
   - JSON request/response format

---

## 2. Data Flow Analysis

### 2.1 End-to-End Request Flow: API Gateway to Texture Service

Based on the `data_flow.puml` and code analysis:

```

#### 3.2.1 Dual-Protocol Server in Texture Service

Modify `services/texture-service/src/main.c` to support both HTTP and gRPC simultaneously:

```c
// main.c - Conceptual structure for dual-protocol support
#include <grpc/grpc.h>
#include <microhttpd.h>

typedef struct {
    struct MHD_Daemon *http_daemon;  // Existing HTTP server
    grpc_server *grpc_server;        // New gRPC server
    ServiceConfig cfg;
} DualServiceCtx;

// gRPC handler (new)
static grpc_call_error handle_grpc_get_texture(
    grpc_call *call,
    GetTextureRequest *req,
    GetTextureResponse **resp
) {
    // Reuse existing handler logic
    TextureError err = {0};
    Texture *tx = texture_handler_get(g_handler, req->texture_id, &err);
    if (!tx) {
        return grpc_call_error_from_texture_error(&err);
    }
    *resp = texture_to_grpc_response(tx);
    texture_free(tx);
    return GRPC_CALL_OK;
}

int main(void)
{
    DualServiceCtx svc;
    config_set_defaults(&svc.cfg);

    // Start HTTP daemon on default port (existing)
    svc.http_daemon = MHD_start_daemon(
        MHD_USE_AUTO | MHD_USE_INTERNAL_POLLING_THREAD,
        svc.cfg.port,
        NULL, NULL,
        request_router, &svc,
        MHD_OPTION_END
    );

    // Start gRPC server on separate port (new)
    uint16_t grpc_port = svc.cfg.port + 1000;  // e.g., 9080 if HTTP is 8080
    svc.grpc_server = grpc_server_create(NULL, NULL);
    grpc_server_add_http2_port(svc.grpc_server,
        format_address("0.0.0.0", grpc_port),
        grpc_insecure_server_credentials_create());

    grpc_server_register_method(svc.grpc_server,
        "/texture.v1.TextureService/GetTexture",
        handle_grpc_get_texture);

    grpc_server_start(svc.grpc_server);

    log_msg(LOG_INFO, "Dual-protocol server: HTTP=%u, gRPC=%u",
            svc.cfg.port, grpc_port);

    // Event loop handles both...
    while (!g_shutdown) sleep(1);

    // Graceful shutdown
    MHD_stop_daemon(svc.http_daemon);
    grpc_server_shutdown_and_notify(svc.grpc_server, NULL, NULL);
    grpc_server_destroy(svc.grpc_server);

    return EXIT_SUCCESS;
}
```

```c

   - Route 5% of traffic to gRPC via feature flag

2. **Incremental Traffic Shift**
   - Week 5: 5% → 25% → 50%
   - Week 6: 75% → 100%

3. **Rollback Capability**

1. Remove HTTP endpoints from texture-service
2. Update API Gateway to use gRPC exclusively for texture-service
3. Remove dual-protocol code, simplify to gRPC-only
4. Repeat phases 2-4 for remaining services (palette, audio, narrative)

```
┌──────────┐   GraphQL/REST   ┌─────────────┐      gRPC/HTTP/2      ┌─────────────────┐
│  Client  │ ───────────────▶ │ API Gateway │ ─────────────────────▶ │ Texture Service │
│          │                  │             │                        │                 │
└──────────┘                  └─────────────┘                        └─────────────────┘
                                    │
                                    │ Protocol Buffer
                                    │ serialization
                                    ▼
                              ┌─────────────┐
                              │ gRPC Client │
                              │   Stub      │
                              └─────────────┘
```

**Key Changes:**
- Binary Protocol Buffers replace JSON serialization
- HTTP/2 enables multiplexed streaming
- Strongly-typed service contracts via `.proto` files
- Built-in load balancing and service discovery options

---

## 4. Risk Assessment

### 4.1 Build System Complexity (HIGH RISK)

**Risk:** Integrating gRPC into a C project via CMake is non-trivial.

**Details:**
- gRPC's primary support is for C++, not C. The C bindings (gRPC Core) have limited documentation.
- Protobuf code generation requires `protobuf-c` (a third-party C protobuf library), not the official Google `protobuf`.
- FetchContent patterns for gRPC often fail due to complex transitive dependencies (abseil, re2, etc.).

**Mitigation:**
- Use system-installed gRPC packages (`apt install libgrpc-dev`) rather than FetchContent
- Consider gRPC-C alternatives like `grpc_c` or `protobuf-c-rpc`
- Extensive pre-merge CI testing of the build system changes

**Evidence from codebase:**
- Current CMakeLists.txt files use FetchContent for simple dependencies (cJSON, libgraphqlparser)
- No existing gRPC usage means no precedent for integration patterns

### 4.2 Performance Characteristics (MEDIUM RISK)

**Risk:** gRPC may introduce latency overhead compared to the custom IPC.

**Details:**
- The current `sc_ipc` library uses UNIX domain sockets with a lean 4-byte length-prefix framing
- gRPC adds HTTP/2 framing, Protobuf encoding/decoding, and connection management overhead
- For intra-host communication, UNIX sockets typically outperform TCP-based gRPC

**Mitigation:**
- Benchmark before/after migration (target: <10% latency increase)
- Consider gRPC over UNIX domain sockets for intra-host calls
- Use connection pooling and keep-alive to reduce handshake overhead

**Evidence from codebase:**
- `sc_ipc.h:62-64` specifies max 64KB payloads and UNIX domain socket transport
- Performance target from `overview.md:17`: "Frame-accurate (< 16 ms) responses"

### 4.3 Operational Complexity During Transition (MEDIUM RISK)

**Risk:** Running dual-protocol systems increases operational burden.

**Details:**
- Configuration management doubles (HTTP endpoints + gRPC ports)
- Monitoring/alerting must cover both protocols
- Load balancers need updating (gRPC requires HTTP/2-aware proxying)
- Service discovery must track additional endpoints

**Mitigation:**
- Use environment variables with sensible defaults
- Implement health checks for both protocols
- Phase transition quickly (6-8 weeks target)
- Document operational runbooks for both modes

**Evidence from codebase:**
- Service configuration via environment variables (`sc_config.h:204-215`)
- Health endpoint pattern: `/healthz` returns `{"status":"ok"}` (`main.c:289-294`)

### 4.4 Code Divergence Risk (MEDIUM RISK)

**Risk:** Maintaining two code paths during migration introduces bugs.

**Details:**
- Handler logic may drift between HTTP and gRPC implementations
- Testing burden doubles
- Edge case handling may differ

**Mitigation:**
- Extract business logic into protocol-agnostic functions
- Comprehensive integration tests covering both paths
- Short migration window to minimize divergence period

**Evidence from codebase:**
- Handler logic in `texture_handler.c` is already separated from transport
- Repository pattern (`texture_repository.c`) enables clean abstraction

### 4.5 Language Ecosystem Friction (LOW-MEDIUM RISK)

**Risk:** gRPC's C ecosystem is less mature than C++ or Go.

**Details:**
- gRPC Core's C API is marked as "low-level" and "for advanced users"
- Documentation focuses on C++, Python, and Go
- Fewer community examples and Stack Overflow answers
- May need to drop to gRPC Core primitives for advanced features

**Mitigation:**
- Consider a C++ wrapper layer if C API proves too cumbersome
- Evaluate alternatives like Cap'n Proto or FlatBuffers with RPC
- Budget extra time for learning curve

**Evidence from documentation:**
- `microservices.md` references gRPC but with C++ style (`gen/palette.grpc.pb-c.h`)
- Current code uses C11/C17 standards without C++ dependencies

---

## 5. Recommendations

### 5.1 Immediate Actions

1. **Proof of Concept:** Build a minimal gRPC server in C for the texture service to validate CMake integration
2. **Benchmark Baseline:** Measure current HTTP latency, throughput, and resource usage
3. **Define Proto Contracts:** Create `.proto` files for all four services before implementation begins

### 5.2 Technical Decisions

1. **Use `grpc-c` or gRPC Core + protobuf-c** for C compatibility
2. **Keep HTTP as fallback** even post-migration for debugging and legacy clients
3. **Implement gRPC reflection** to enable tools like `grpcurl` for debugging

### 5.3 Success Criteria

| Metric | Target |
|--------|--------|
| P95 Latency | < 20ms (max 25% increase from baseline) |
| Error Rate | < 0.1% during rollout |
| Migration Duration | < 8 weeks per service |
| Build Time Increase | < 20% |

    // Start HTTP daemon on default port (existing)
    svc.http_daemon = MHD_start_daemon(
        MHD_USE_AUTO | MHD_USE_INTERNAL_POLLING_THREAD,
        svc.cfg.port,
        NULL, NULL,
        request_router, &svc,
        MHD_OPTION_END
    );

    // Start gRPC server on separate port (new)
    uint16_t grpc_port = svc.cfg.port + 1000;  // e.g., 9080 if HTTP is 8080
    svc.grpc_server = grpc_server_create(NULL, NULL);
    grpc_server_add_http2_port(svc.grpc_server,
        format_address("0.0.0.0", grpc_port),
        grpc_insecure_server_credentials_create());

    grpc_server_register_method(svc.grpc_server,
        "/texture.v1.TextureService/GetTexture",
        handle_grpc_get_texture);

    grpc_server_start(svc.grpc_server);

    log_msg(LOG_INFO, "Dual-protocol server: HTTP=%u, gRPC=%u",
            svc.cfg.port, grpc_port);

    // Event loop handles both...
    while (!g_shutdown) sleep(1);

    // Graceful shutdown
    MHD_stop_daemon(svc.http_daemon);
    grpc_server_shutdown_and_notify(svc.grpc_server, NULL, NULL);
    grpc_server_destroy(svc.grpc_server);

    return EXIT_SUCCESS;
}
```

#### 3.3.1 Deployment Strategy

1. **Canary Deployment**

2. **Incremental Traffic Shift**
   - Week 5: 5% → 25% → 50%
   - Week 6: 75% → 100%
   - Monitor latency, error rates, resource usage

3. **Rollback Capability**
   - `SC_USE_GRPC_TEXTURE=false` instantly reverts to HTTP
   - No service restart required

### 3.4 Phase 4: Consolidation (Weeks 7-8)

1. Remove HTTP endpoints from texture-service
2. Update API Gateway to use gRPC exclusively for texture-service
3. Remove dual-protocol code, simplify to gRPC-only
4. Repeat phases 2-4 for remaining services (palette, audio, narrative)

### 3.5 New gRPC-Based Communication Flow

```
┌──────────┐   GraphQL/REST   ┌─────────────┐      gRPC/HTTP/2      ┌─────────────────┐
│  Client  │ ───────────────▶ │ API Gateway │ ─────────────────────▶ │ Texture Service │
│          │                  │             │                        │                 │
└──────────┘                  └─────────────┘                        └─────────────────┘

                                    ▼

                              │ gRPC Client │
                              │   Stub      │
                              └─────────────┘
```

**Key Changes:**
- Binary Protocol Buffers replace JSON serialization
- HTTP/2 enables multiplexed streaming
- Strongly-typed service contracts via `.proto` files
- Built-in load balancing and service discovery options

## 4. Risk Assessment

### 4.1 Build System Complexity (HIGH RISK)

**Risk:** Integrating gRPC into a C project via CMake is non-trivial.

**Details:**
- gRPC's primary support is for C++, not C. The C bindings (gRPC Core) have limited documentation.
- Protobuf code generation requires `protobuf-c` (a third-party C protobuf library), not the official Google `protobuf`.
- FetchContent patterns for gRPC often fail due to complex transitive dependencies (abseil, re2, etc.).

**Mitigation:**
- Use system-installed gRPC packages (`apt install libgrpc-dev`) rather than FetchContent
- Consider gRPC-C alternatives like `grpc_c` or `protobuf-c-rpc`
- Extensive pre-merge CI testing of the build system changes

**Evidence from codebase:**
- Current CMakeLists.txt files use FetchContent for simple dependencies (cJSON, libgraphqlparser)
- No existing gRPC usage means no precedent for integration patterns

### 4.2 Performance Characteristics (MEDIUM RISK)

**Risk:** gRPC may introduce latency overhead compared to the custom IPC.

**Details:**
- The current `sc_ipc` library uses UNIX domain sockets with a lean 4-byte length-prefix framing
- gRPC adds HTTP/2 framing, Protobuf encoding/decoding, and connection management overhead
- For intra-host communication, UNIX sockets typically outperform TCP-based gRPC

**Mitigation:**
- Benchmark before/after migration (target: <10% latency increase)
- Consider gRPC over UNIX domain sockets for intra-host calls
- Use connection pooling and keep-alive to reduce handshake overhead

**Evidence from codebase:**
- `sc_ipc.h:62-64` specifies max 64KB payloads and UNIX domain socket transport
- Performance target from `overview.md:17`: "Frame-accurate (< 16 ms) responses"

### 4.3 Operational Complexity During Transition (MEDIUM RISK)

**Risk:** Running dual-protocol systems increases operational burden.

**Details:**
- Configuration management doubles (HTTP endpoints + gRPC ports)
- Monitoring/alerting must cover both protocols
- Load balancers need updating (gRPC requires HTTP/2-aware proxying)
- Service discovery must track additional endpoints

**Mitigation:**
- Use environment variables with sensible defaults
- Implement health checks for both protocols
- Phase transition quickly (6-8 weeks target)
- Document operational runbooks for both modes

**Evidence from codebase:**
- Service configuration via environment variables (`sc_config.h:204-215`)
- Health endpoint pattern: `/healthz` returns `{"status":"ok"}` (`main.c:289-294`)

### 4.4 Code Divergence Risk (MEDIUM RISK)

**Risk:** Maintaining two code paths during migration introduces bugs.

**Details:**
- Handler logic may drift between HTTP and gRPC implementations
- Testing burden doubles
- Edge case handling may differ

**Mitigation:**
- Extract business logic into protocol-agnostic functions
- Comprehensive integration tests covering both paths
- Short migration window to minimize divergence period

**Evidence from codebase:**
- Handler logic in `texture_handler.c` is already separated from transport
- Repository pattern (`texture_repository.c`) enables clean abstraction

### 4.5 Language Ecosystem Friction (LOW-MEDIUM RISK)

**Risk:** gRPC's C ecosystem is less mature than C++ or Go.

**Details:**
- gRPC Core's C API is marked as "low-level" and "for advanced users"
- Documentation focuses on C++, Python, and Go
- Fewer community examples and Stack Overflow answers
- May need to drop to gRPC Core primitives for advanced features

**Mitigation:**
- Consider a C++ wrapper layer if C API proves too cumbersome
- Evaluate alternatives like Cap'n Proto or FlatBuffers with RPC
- Budget extra time for learning curve

**Evidence from documentation:**
- `microservices.md` references gRPC but with C++ style (`gen/palette.grpc.pb-c.h`)
- Current code uses C11/C17 standards without C++ dependencies

---

## 5. Recommendations

### 5.1 Immediate Actions

1. **Proof of Concept:** Build a minimal gRPC server in C for the texture service to validate CMake integration
2. **Benchmark Baseline:** Measure current HTTP latency, throughput, and resource usage
3. **Define Proto Contracts:** Create `.proto` files for all four services before implementation begins

### 5.2 Technical Decisions

1. **Use `grpc-c` or gRPC Core + protobuf-c** for C compatibility
2. **Keep HTTP as fallback** even post-migration for debugging and legacy clients
3. **Implement gRPC reflection** to enable tools like `grpcurl` for debugging

### 5.3 Success Criteria

| Metric | Target |
|--------|--------|
| P95 Latency | < 20ms (max 25% increase from baseline) |
| Error Rate | < 0.1% during rollout |
| Migration Duration | < 8 weeks per service |
| Build Time Increase | < 20% |

## Executive Summary

This report provides a comprehensive architectural analysis of the SynestheticCanvas API Suite's inter-service communication system and outlines a phased migration plan from the current hybrid IPC/HTTP approach to gRPC. ## 1. Component Identification

### 1.1 Current Inter-Service Communication Components

The SynestheticCanvas system uses a **hybrid communication approach**:

1. **UNIX Domain Socket IPC Library (`sc_ipc`)** - for local inter-process communication
2. **HTTP/REST over libcurl** - for network-based service-to-service calls

### 1.2 Current Communication Architecture

1. **The `sc_ipc` library** provides UNIX domain socket communication with:

3. **Microservices** expose HTTP endpoints via libmicrohttpd:

- Consider gRPC over UNIX domain sockets for intra-host calls
- Use connection pooling and keep-alive to reduce handshake overhead

**Evidence from codebase:**
- `sc_ipc.h:62-64` specifies max 64KB payloads and UNIX domain socket transport
- Performance target from `overview.md:17`: "Frame-accurate (< 16 ms) responses"

### 4.3 Operational Complexity During Transition (MEDIUM RISK)

**Risk:** Running dual-protocol systems increases operational burden.

**Details:**
- Configuration management doubles (HTTP endpoints + gRPC ports)
- Monitoring/alerting must cover both protocols
- Load balancers need updating (gRPC requires HTTP/2-aware proxying)
- Service discovery must track additional endpoints

**Mitigation:**
- Use environment variables with sensible defaults
- Implement health checks for both protocols
- Phase transition quickly (6-8 weeks target)
- Document operational runbooks for both modes

**Evidence from codebase:**
- Service configuration via environment variables (`sc_config.h:204-215`)
- Health endpoint pattern: `/healthz` returns `{"status":"ok"}` (`main.c:289-294`)

### 4.4 Code Divergence Risk (MEDIUM RISK)

**Risk:** Maintaining two code paths during migration introduces bugs.

**Details:**
- Handler logic may drift between HTTP and gRPC implementations
- Testing burden doubles
- Edge case handling may differ

**Mitigation:**
- Extract business logic into protocol-agnostic functions
- Comprehensive integration tests covering both paths
- Short migration window to minimize divergence period

**Evidence from codebase:**
- Handler logic in `texture_handler.c` is already separated from transport
- Repository pattern (`texture_repository.c`) enables clean abstraction

### 4.5 Language Ecosystem Friction (LOW-MEDIUM RISK)

**Risk:** gRPC's C ecosystem is less mature than C++ or Go.

**Details:**
- gRPC Core's C API is marked as "low-level" and "for advanced users"
- Documentation focuses on C++, Python, and Go
- Fewer community examples and Stack Overflow answers
- May need to drop to gRPC Core primitives for advanced features

**Mitigation:**
- Consider a C++ wrapper layer if C API proves too cumbersome
- Evaluate alternatives like Cap'n Proto or FlatBuffers with RPC
- Budget extra time for learning curve

**Evidence from documentation:**
- `microservices.md` references gRPC but with C++ style (`gen/palette.grpc.pb-c.h`)
- Current code uses C11/C17 standards without C++ dependencies

---

## 5. Recommendations

### 5.1 Immediate Actions

1. **Proof of Concept:** Build a minimal gRPC server in C for the texture service to validate CMake integration
2. **Benchmark Baseline:** Measure current HTTP latency, throughput, and resource usage
3. **Define Proto Contracts:** Create `.proto` files for all four services before implementation begins

### 5.2 Technical Decisions

1. **Use `grpc-c` or gRPC Core + protobuf-c** for C compatibility
2. **Keep HTTP as fallback** even post-migration for debugging and legacy clients
3. **Implement gRPC reflection** to enable tools like `grpcurl` for debugging

### 5.3 Success Criteria

| Metric | Target |
|--------|--------|
| P95 Latency | < 20ms (max 25% increase from baseline) |
| Error Rate | < 0.1% during rollout |
| Migration Duration | < 8 weeks per service |
| Build Time Increase | < 20% |

---

## 6. Appendix: File Reference Summary

### IPC/Communication Layer
- `libs/sc_ipc/include/sc_ipc.h` - IPC API (lines 1-207)
- `libs/sc_ipc/src/sc_ipc.c` - IPC implementation (lines 1-443)
- `api-gateway/src/services/service_client.c` - HTTP client (lines 1-359)

### API Gateway
- `api-gateway/graphql/resolvers.c` - GraphQL resolvers (lines 1-408)
- `api-gateway/src/main.c` - Gateway entry point
- `api-gateway/CMakeLists.txt` - Build configuration (lines 1-281)

### Texture Service (Representative Microservice)
- `services/texture-service/src/main.c` - Service entry point (lines 1-438)
- `services/texture-service/src/texture_handler.c` - Request handlers (lines 1-400)
- `services/texture-service/CMakeLists.txt` - Build configuration (lines 1-248)

### Configuration/Common
- `libs/sc_common/include/sc_config.h` - Configuration framework (lines 1-303)
- `docs/architecture/data_flow.puml` - Architecture diagram (lines 1-172)
- `docs/architecture/overview.md` - System overview (lines 1-299)
- `docs/architecture/microservices.md` - Microservices documentation (lines 1-572)
